section.heading: Organization of a government pursuant to a constitution

content.p: Fully recognizing the principle of government by consent, sections 731b to 731e of this title are now adopted in the nature of a compact so that the people of Puerto Rico may organize a government pursuant to a constitution of their own adoption.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was not enacted as part of the Puerto Rican Federal Relations Act which comprises this chapter.

note.heading: Repeals
note.p: Act July 3, 1950, ch. 446, § 6, 64 Stat. 320, provided that: “All laws or parts of laws inconsistent with this Act [enacting sections 731b to 731e of this title] are hereby repealed.”
